message.thinking:
We need to evaluate the given text against three questions: Chapters, Description, Structure. Provide JSON array of 3 objects. Each object: "question" field should be one word: "Chapters", "Description", "Structure" accordingly. "feedback" field: text highlighting problems or strengths. "complies": boolean.

We need to analyze the text.

The text is a section of thesis introduction. It includes a title "1. DARBA BŪTĪBA UN AKTUALITĀTE" (Work significance and relevance). It has content about problem statement, topic relevance, etc. Then there is "1.1. tabula" which seems to be a table of the bachelor thesis development plan. Then it lists activities and dates. Then it lists some tasks.

The question 1: Chapters. Does the text list the main sections of the thesis? The text does not list the main sections; it only lists a section 1. and 1.1. It doesn't list chapters like "Introduction", "Literature Review", "Methodology", etc. So it does not comply. Feedback: mention missing list of main sections.

Question 2: Description. Does the text briefly describe contents of each section? It does describe content of section 1, but not of other sections. So partially but not fully. But question: "Does the text briefly describe contents of each section." Since it only has one section, maybe it's okay? But likely they expect description of each section. It only has one section, so not enough. So not fully compliant. Provide feedback.

Question 3: Structure. Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text? No, because only one section is present. So not easy. Provide feedback.

Thus all three are false compliance.

But maybe we can say "Chapters" not present. "Description" partially but not all. "Structure" not clear. So all false.

Let's produce JSON array accordingly.
